item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche tells Meet the Press the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey goes beyond an Instagram post showing seashells arranged to say “86 47,” terminology the Justice Department says threatens the president’s life.

media:content.media:title: Acting AG Todd Blanche says Comey indictment looks beyond ‘a single Instagram post’
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche tells Meet the Press the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey goes beyond an Instagram post showing seashells arranged to say “86 47,” terminology the Justice Department says threatens the president’s life.

item.title: Acting AG Todd Blanche says Comey indictment looks beyond ‘a single Instagram post’

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: Cherie DeVaux became the first woman to train the winning horse at the Kentucky Derby when Golden Tempo claimed the 152nd Run for the Roses on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Cherie DeVaux smiles while holding a trophy surrounded by other people.
media:content.media:description: Cherie DeVaux, trainer of Golden Tempo #19, celebrates with the trophy in the winner's circle following the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on May 2, 2026.

media:content.media:text: 152nd Kentucky Derby
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz rode Golden Tempo to a first-place finish at Churchill Downs on Saturday.

item.title: Kentucky Derby winner Cherie DeVaux bet on herself and won big

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Once again, tens of thousands flocked to Churchill Downs, dressed to the nines, to partake in one of the grandest spectacles in sport and, perhaps, travel back in time.

media:content.media:text: Overall view of race track.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: A huge crowd at Churchill Downs for the Kentucky Derby.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Race fans pass by the Pat Day statue.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Fans look over the program.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Horses break from the starting gate.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: A composite image of people at the Kentucky Derby.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Three men cheer while standing in the infield of Churchill Downs under gray skies.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Horses take off from the starting gate.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: A man smokes a cigar while watching the fourth race.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: People wear Derby hats.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Attendees check racing forms of the prep races.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: 152nd Kentucky Derby
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: A jockey holds up a trophy while smiling broadly, surrounded by other celebrants.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Red roses appear in the foreground before the twin spires of Churchill Downs.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

item.title: Photos from the 152nd Kentucky Derby: The best day in horse racing, the 'sport of kings'

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Get the latest ahed of the 2026 Kentucky Derby, including how to watch, what time it starts, where to watch or stream the race, who is expected to win and more.

media:content.media:text: A blurred view of a horse and jockey racing down the track.
media:content.media:description: The Puma trains on the track during morning workouts ahead of the running of the 152nd Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on April 29, 2026.

item.title: 2026 Kentucky Derby guide: Time, TV channels, betting favorites and more

item.description: Spirit Airlines announced in a statement that it would wind down operations immediately, canceling all flights, over rising jet fuel costs and struggling with profitability. NBC News’ Jay Blackman reports on how the decision will impact thousands of employees and travelers.

media:content.media:title: Spirit Airlines announces all flights are canceled as it shuts down operations
media:content.media:description: Spirit Airlines announced in a statement that it would wind down operations immediately, canceling all flights, over rising jet fuel costs and struggling with profitability. NBC News’ Jay Blackman reports on how the decision will impact thousands of employees and travelers.

item.title: Spirit Airlines announces all flights are canceled as it shuts down operations

item.dc:creator: Jay Ganglani
item.description: A photo exhibition offering a rare look into industrial fishing in Southeast Asia — and its impact on fragile marine ecosystems — was on display at a New York gallery last month.

media:content.media:text: Tanjung Luar is one of the largest shark markets in Indonesia and Southeast Asia, from where shark fins are exported to other Asian markets, primarily Hong Kong and China, was bones are used in cosmetic products also sold to China. Shark meat and skins are consumed locally as an important source of protein. In recent years, facing heavy criticism because of the unregulated shark fishing industry, the Indonesian government has sought to bring in stricter controls over commercial hunting of sharks in an attempt to balance the needs of fishermen as well as the need to protect dwindling shark populations.
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Many filipino commercial and small-scale fishers are being chased or hassled by Chinese Coast Guard, Navy, and militia ships while out at sea. Previously, attacks or harassment in this area did not exist, but was well-documented in other areas. Since August 2024, incidences of harassment have started and increased since. Many Filipino fishermen believe that the Chinese are now moving to build outposts in the nearby Sabina Shoal to dominate both trade routes and the fishing industry.
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

item.title: Photo essay: Southeast Asia's overfishing crisis — and the effort to uncover its roots

item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche tells “Meet the Press” that the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey “is not just about a single Instagram post” and investigators have been collecting evidence for nearly a year.

media:content.media:title: Acting AG Blanche says Comey indictment ‘not just about’ one post
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche tells “Meet the Press” that the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey “is not just about a single Instagram post” and investigators have been collecting evidence for nearly a year.

item.title: Acting AG Blanche says Comey indictment ‘not just about’ one post

item.description: First horse trained by a woman wins at Kentucky Derby

media:content.media:title: First horse trained by a woman wins at Kentucky Derby
media:content.media:description: First horse trained by a woman wins at Kentucky Derby

item.title: First horse trained by a woman wins at Kentucky Derby

item.title: Aziz Ansari debuts as FBI Director Kash Patel on 'SNL'

item.description: Man says Waymo drove away with his luggage

item.title: Man says Waymo drove away with his luggage

item.description: Transportation Sec. Sean Duffy tells reporters that the collapse of Spirit Arlines was years in the making, blaming the Biden administration’s denial of a merger with JetBlue in 2024, and not the recent Iran war, which has sent jet fuel costs soaring.

media:content.media:title: Trump admin says Iran war not to blame for Spirit shutdown
media:content.media:description: Transportation Sec. Sean Duffy tells reporters that the collapse of Spirit Arlines was years in the making, blaming the Biden administration’s denial of a merger with JetBlue in 2024, and not the recent Iran war, which has sent jet fuel costs soaring.

item.title: Trump admin says Iran war not to blame for Spirit shutdown

item.description: Duffy explains why there was no bailout of Spirit

media:content.media:title: Duffy explains why there was no bailout of Spirit
media:content.media:description: Duffy explains why there was no bailout of Spirit

item.title: Duffy explains why there was no bailout of Spirit

item.description: Passengers learn of Spirit's shutdown at the airport

media:content.media:title: Passengers learn of Spirit's shutdown at the airport
media:content.media:description: Passengers learn of Spirit's shutdown at the airport

item.title: Passengers learn of Spirit's shutdown at the airport

item.description: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany

media:content.media:title: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany
media:content.media:description: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany

item.title: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: A popular local athletic club is closed in Portland, Oregon, after a man drove a car full of explosives and propane into the building on Saturday

media:content.media:text: A police car parked on a street behind police tape
media:content.media:description: Police shut down the block surrounding the Multnomah Athletic Club in Portland, Ore., on Saturday.

item.title: Man drives car full of explosives into a Portland athletic club

item.dc:creator: Hannah Peart
item.description: Two top Republican lawmakers expressed their concern Saturday about President Donald Trump’s decision to withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from NATO ally Germany.

media:content.media:text: Two armed soldiers walk in front of a green vehicle on a road
media:content.media:description: The unmanned grounded vehicle Themis of Milrem Robotics during the "xTech|Edge Strike: Ground" technology evaluation exercise hosted by the US Army's 2nd Cavalry Regiment at the U.S. military Grafenwoehr Training Area in Grafenwoehr, Germany, on March 13, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Senate Votes 4/3/25
media:content.media:description: Sen. Roger Wicker, R-Miss., expressed concern Saturday about President Donald Trump's decision to withdraw troops from Germany, in a joint statement with Rep. Mike Rogers, R-Ala.

media:content.media:text: Mike Rogers, R-Ala., in the U.S. Capitol after the House passed the National Defense Authorization Act on July 14, 2023.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Mike Rogers, R-Ala.

item.title: Top Republicans warn Trump's Germany troop withdrawals send wrong message to Putin

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said Sunday that the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey goes beyond the Instagram post central to his case.“This is not just about a single Instagram post,” Blanche told NBC News’ “Meet the Press” on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: Key Speakers At The Conservative Political Action Conference
media:content.media:description: Todd Blanche, US deputy attorney general, speaks during the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Grapevine, Texas, US, on Thursday, March 26, 2026.

media:content.media:text: A federal grand jury has returned an indictment charging former FBI Director James Comey over an Instagram photo he posted of seashells spelling out a message about President Donald Trump last year.
media:content.media:description: A federal grand jury has returned an indictment charging former FBI Director James Comey over an Instagram photo he posted of seashells spelling out a message about President Donald Trump last year.

item.title: Acting attorney general says indictment against James Comey goes beyond seashell photo

item.dc:creator: Jay Ganglani
item.description: A photo exhibition offering a rare look into industrial fishing in Southeast Asia — and its impact on fragile marine ecosystems — was on display at a New York gallery last month.

media:content.media:text: Tanjung Luar is one of the largest shark markets in Indonesia and Southeast Asia, from where shark fins are exported to other Asian markets, primarily Hong Kong and China, was bones are used in cosmetic products also sold to China. Shark meat and skins are consumed locally as an important source of protein. In recent years, facing heavy criticism because of the unregulated shark fishing industry, the Indonesian government has sought to bring in stricter controls over commercial hunting of sharks in an attempt to balance the needs of fishermen as well as the need to protect dwindling shark populations.
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Many filipino commercial and small-scale fishers are being chased or hassled by Chinese Coast Guard, Navy, and militia ships while out at sea. Previously, attacks or harassment in this area did not exist, but was well-documented in other areas. Since August 2024, incidences of harassment have started and increased since. Many Filipino fishermen believe that the Chinese are now moving to build outposts in the nearby Sabina Shoal to dominate both trade routes and the fishing industry.
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

item.dc:creator: Dareh Gregorian
Megan Shannon
item.description: The Trump administration has made an aggressive push to add the president's name to buildings, battleships, money and government websites.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: DONALD J. TRUMP etched into a wet stone facade, with snow covering the grass beneath it.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Workers in two hydraulic cranes are suspended above the ground in front of the Kennedy Center's facade, with a giant purple tarp stretched behind them.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump stands next to a signboard with an image of a battleship inside a room at Mar-a-Lago.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Designs for Semiquincentennial gold coins featuring President Trump.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: A 50 dollar bill poking up from a seat pocket in President Trump's pants as he ascends the stairs to Air Force One.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: The State Department will be releasing a limited series of U.S. passports featuring the image of President Trump, a State Department official said
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Image: Workers install a new banner featuring an image of President Donald Trump on the facade of the U.S. Department of Justice
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump speaks at at a "Trump Accounts" event on Jan. 28, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Announces TrumpRx From The White House
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

item.title: 12 places Trump's name or image is being added by the federal government
